purpose: Measured mutation-score floors for the hand-written modules Stryker mutates (generated src/** excluded). Floors ratchet monotonic-up; raise only after a real test improvement. Governed modules fail closed when a report contains zero covered mutants. Stryker uses aggregate coverage plus Vitest related-test selection because per-test mutant attribution is unreliable for composed-fetch on Linux; the dates property suite uses per-assertion fixed fast-check options so its score is repeatable. Phase 1 covers the wrapper hand-written modules. Phase 2 adds the MCP safety-critical modules (confirmation token store, centralized guarded registration, and governed risk registry) — Stryker's vitest-runner accepts vitest >=2.0.0, so the MCP suite is mutated on its now-unified Vitest 4 toolchain. dates.ts and webhook-url.ts floors were raised after Item 3 added month/weekday-name parse tests and the fe80::/10 [fec0::1]/[febf::1] boundary asserts.
